title: Sleep-disordered breathing ENT documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

investigations.text: Supports documenting sleep symptoms and selected comorbidity context in children without diagnosing obstructive sleep apnoea or ordering polysomnography.
investigations.rationale: The exact paediatric section supports caregiver-reported sleep-disordered breathing and selected comorbidity context plus clinician-performed ENT assessment; it does not recommend surgery or testing.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documenting sleep symptoms and selected comorbidity context in children without diagnosing obstructive sleep apnoea or ordering polysomnography.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Tonsillectomy in Children (Update)

exact_section.heading: Key action statements 4–6 — sleep-disordered breathing and comorbidity context

treatment_and_management.text: Supports tailored follow-up, CPAP review, annual review after optimisation, symptoms, Epworth score, vigilance and driving, AHI or ODI, adherence, treatment experience, sleep hygiene, and clinician-reviewed sleep-study follow-up.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact adult follow-up section supports recording a clinician-entered plan, safety discussion, arranged follow-up, and questions only when actually documented.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports tailored follow-up, CPAP review, annual review after optimisation, symptoms, Epworth score, vigilance and driving, AHI or ODI, adherence, treatment experience, sleep hygiene, and clinician-reviewed sleep-study follow-up.

source.title: Obstructive sleep apnoea/hypopnoea syndrome and obesity hypoventilation syndrome in over 16s

referral.text: Supports snoring, witnessed apnoeas, unrefreshing or fragmented sleep, waking headache, sleepiness or fatigue, nocturia, choking, cognitive symptoms, obesity context, Epworth Sleepiness Scale, and STOP-Bang, while prohibiting use of Epworth alone to determine referral.
referral.rationale: The exact adult OSAHS assessment section supports documenting snoring, witnessed pauses, choking, sleep quality, waking symptoms, daytime impact, functional safety context, obesity and comorbidity context without diagnosing sleep apnoea or calculating a score.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports snoring, witnessed apnoeas, unrefreshing or fragmented sleep, waking headache, sleepiness or fatigue, nocturia, choking, cognitive symptoms, obesity context, Epworth Sleepiness Scale, and STOP-Bang, while prohibiting use of Epworth alone to determine referral.

source.title: Obstructive sleep apnoea/hypopnoea syndrome and obesity hypoventilation syndrome in over 16s

sources_used.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Tonsillectomy in Children (Update)
sources_used.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE paediatric ENT, anaesthesia, analgesia, and postoperative adaptation

sources_used.title: Obstructive sleep apnoea/hypopnoea syndrome and obesity hypoventilation syndrome in over 16s

source_limitations: The workflow does not distinguish adult from paediatric presentation, established OSAHS from undifferentiated symptoms, or device follow-up from initial ENT review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
